# UC-13.2.27 · Observability Cloud Alert Ingestion

## Description

Ingesting Splunk Observability Cloud alerts into ITSI unifies cloud-native and on-prem monitoring into a single episode management workflow, eliminating tool-switching.

## Value

Ingesting Splunk Observability Cloud alerts into ITSI unifies cloud-native and on-prem monitoring into a single episode management workflow, eliminating tool-switching.

## Implementation

Configure Observability Cloud to forward alerts via webhook to Splunk HEC. Normalize alert payloads into the ITSI Universal Alerting schema. Create a Universal Correlation Search to convert incoming alerts into notable events. Configure NEAPs to group O11y alerts with infrastructure alerts into unified episodes. Track alert volume and false positive rate.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI, Splunk Observability Cloud.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Observability Cloud webhooks, `itsi_tracked_alerts`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Configure Observability Cloud to forward alerts via webhook to Splunk HEC. Normalize alert payloads into the ITSI Universal Alerting schema. Create a Universal Correlation Search to convert incoming alerts into notable events. Configure NEAPs to group O11y alerts with infrastructure alerts into unified episodes. Track alert volume and false positive rate.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsi_tracked_alerts source="*observability*" OR source="*o11y*"
| stats count by service_name severity source
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Observability Cloud Alert Ingestion** — Ingesting Splunk Observability Cloud alerts into ITSI unifies cloud-native and on-prem monitoring into a single episode management workflow, eliminating tool-switching.

Documented **Data sources**: Observability Cloud webhooks, `itsi_tracked_alerts`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI, Splunk Observability Cloud. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsi_tracked_alerts.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsi_tracked_alerts. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by service_name severity source** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.



Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when you know the service or episode context. Compare with the ITSI Service Analyzer, episode or notable views, and Glass Tables or drill-downs for the same time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (O11y alert source, count, severity), Time chart (alert volume over time).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsi_tracked_alerts source="*observability*" OR source="*o11y*"
| stats count by service_name severity source
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (O11y alert source, count, severity), Time chart (alert volume over time).
